input:
When they replied in the affirmative, he said: “By Him who holds my soul in His hand, I knew nothing of this until I heard what you heard. The believers are a single unit. The most humble among them may speak for them all and extend protection on their behalf. We will honour her pledge.” When people left the mosque, the Prophet went to his daughter and told her to be hospitable to Abū al-ʿĀṣ. He made it clear to her that she was not to treat him as her husband despite the fact that they were not divorced.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 27: Family Matters

output.scene_title: The Prophet's Instructions to His Daughter
output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 27: Family Matters

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Early 50s, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a green cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by green cloak and subtle warm light.

characters.name: Fatimah bint Muhammad
characters.appearance: Teenage, light olive skin, slender build, slightly shorter than average. Wearing modest black thobe with white scarf wrapped around her head, simple leather sandals. Gentle features, calm expression.

characters.name: Abū al-ʿĀṣ ibn Rāqīq
characters.appearance: Young adult, medium build, average height. Wearing simple off-white thobe, plain brown woolen cloak, white cloth wrapped as loose head covering. Worn leather sandals. Strong features, neutral expression.

output.moral_lesson: Respecting others' privacy and dignity is essential, even when it comes to family matters.
output.narrative_text: The Prophet went home and told his daughter Fatimah to be kind to Abū al-ʿĀṣ, but made it clear that she should not treat him like a husband, even though they were not divorced.

compliance.notes: Prophet shown from behind, interacting with his daughter. Focus on gentle scene, emphasizing kindness and respect.

output.image_prompt: Watercolor illustration of a modest 7th century Makkah mud-brick home. A man in white thobe and green cloak, white turban, seen entirely from behind, standing in the doorway with warm sunlight streaming behind him. Inside, a teenage girl with light olive skin, slender build, wearing black thobe and white scarf, sitting on a woven mat looking up at the Prophet's figure. Young adult man with medium build, off-white thobe and brown cloak, standing near the door, looking at the Prophet with neutral expression. Simple mud-brick walls, clay water jug in corner. Medium shot, eye level. Warm golden palette. No modern objects.
